description: M-26-14 Cat B/G: DGA detection via DNS query entropy (high_info_content per host, 15m buckets)

detectors.detector_description: Unusually high entropy DNS queries from this host — DGA C2 indicator

custom_settings.m2614_requirement: Appendix B §5(b) Network Sessions/C2 | §5(g) IoC Monitoring (DGA) | Element 3 L4 ML Detection
custom_settings.m2614_note: Datafeed strips Zeek trailing dot from dns.question.name via script_fields. Baseline period: 7 days minimum (15m buckets accumulate data faster than hourly jobs).
custom_settings.m2614_acceptance_criteria: Detects DGA activity within 15m bucket. <=5% false positive rate vs. known-good DNS corpus. Baseline period: 7 days minimum.
custom_settings.m2614_description: M-26-14 Appendix B §5(b)/(g): Detects anomalously high entropy in DNS query names per source host — primary statistical signal for Domain Generation Algorithm (DGA) C2 traffic. No Elastic prebuilt equivalent at per-host partitioning granularity. Complements Category G IoC matching (m2614-appendixb-g-ioc-monitoring.ndjson) for novel DGA domains not yet in threat intelligence feeds. A host generating high-entropy DNS queries in a 15-minute window strongly suggests active DGA malware. high_info_content measures information-theoretic entropy of dns.question.name values per host. Normal traffic to well-known services produces low entropy; DGA names produce high entropy. M-26-14 Appendix B §5(b) network sessions/C2, §5(g) IoC unindexed DGA, Element 3 L4.
